title: How the Heat Is Upending Plans for America’s 250th Birthday
summary: A parade in Philadelphia, one of the largest in its history, was canceled on Friday, and the Great American State Fair on the National Mall in Washington closed for several hours.

title: As Heat Suffocates Washington DC, Calls to Emergency Medical Services Climb
summary: Near 100-degree temperatures were expected to continue as medical workers prepared for more heat-related illnesses during celebrations of the nation’s 250th anniversary.

title: Once the Centerpiece of Celebration, a Faded Declaration Recedes
summary: Sunlight and abuse have taken a toll on the document, encased in bulletproof glass. But the Trump administration “hasn’t put much emphasis on it,” a former archivist notes.

title: Celebrating America’s 250th Anniversary Across the Country
summary: Even as an oppressive heat wave stifled much of the United States, the nationwide party continued.

title: Making a Cake for America’s 250th Birthday
summary: In her quest to mark the nation’s 250th with a unifying dessert, Grace Pak had to navigate the pitfalls of a polarized Washington.

title: Burger vs. Bratwurst: A Climate Guide to Your July 4 Cookout
summary: Beef is far worse for greenhouse gas emissions compared with other meat.

title: Taylor Swift Wears Dior Dress by Jonathan Anderson for MSG Wedding
summary: The pop star said “I do” to a gown designed by Jonathan Anderson, the French house’s creative director.

title: With Its Celebrity Guests, Taylor Swift’s Wedding Rivals the Met Gala
summary: Top-secret celebrity guest lists! No cell phones! Big fashion moments! S.U.V.s galore! New York usually gets only one party like this a year.

title: Putin Visits Battlefield and Vows to Take More of Ukraine
summary: The Russian leader denounced Ukraine’s “imaginary achievements” on the battlefield of late, calling its leaders “play actors.”

title: It’s Day 1 of the Supreme Leader’s Funeral, and Allies Gather in Iran
summary: Foreign dignitaries paid their respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Friday as days of ceremonies got underway.

title: Trump Targets Not Just Georgia’s Vote, but Also Trust in Elections
summary: The president has sent 260 F.B.I. analysts to Georgia, repeating his baseless claims of fraud in 2020. But critics say the intention is to undermine overall confidence in the electoral process.

title: Parents Will Be Able to Enroll Newborns in Trump Accounts at the Hospital
summary: The Social Security Administration will introduce a process to sign up babies for the investment program at the same time they are registered for a Social Security number.

title: The Hottest Stock Markets Lead to the Biggest Losses
summary: Investor enthusiasm culminated in some of the worst cases of wealth destruction in the last 100 years, a long-running study shows.

title: Vicki Gunvalson on Her ‘Real Housewives of Orange County’ Return
summary: When Vicki Gunvalson signed on for “The Real Housewives of Orange County” 20 years ago, no one knew she’d become a quintessential reality TV star.

title: At the L.A. Warehouse Fire, First It Was the Smoke. Now It’s the Smell.
summary: Rotting food — millions of pounds of it — remains in an L.A. cold-storage facility damaged in a fire. Residents say the cleanup hasn’t been fast enough.

title: A Big Gamble for the Left: Can Socialism Appeal in a Swing State?
summary: Francesca Hong, a state legislator running for governor of Wisconsin, wants to prove that a democratic socialist can win in a battleground. Some say she would hand the race to Republicans.

title: Trump Intercedes to Push Robert Smullen Out of N.Y. House Race
summary: Assemblyman Robert Smullen, who lost a Republican primary, will not run on the Conservative Party line in his bid to replace Representative Elise Stefanik.

title: This Pathetic Groveling Is No Way to Rebuild a Party
summary: The Democrats’ constant solicitations for cash do not make their supporters feel like they are on a winning team.

title: The South Korean Mine at the Center of America’s Tungsten Push
summary: Deep beneath a South Korean mountain, workers are reviving a mine that could challenge China’s grip on a metal vital to weapons, chips and industry.
